title: veronica agrestis var. polita
intro: Veronica agrestis var. polita, also known as the Wood Speedwell or Veronica polita, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Veronica agrestis var. polita include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, flu, and other illnesses. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in topical applications, such as salves and creams, to take advantage of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
